# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]
tokio = { version = "1", features = ["full"] }
tokio-rustls = "0.26"
rustls = "0.23"
rustls-pemfile = "2"
thiserror = "2"
rust_decimal = "1"
time = { version = "0.3", features = ["formatting", "parsing", "macros"] }
serde = { version = "1", features = ["derive"] }
sqlx = { version = "0.8", default-features = false, features = ["runtime-tokio"] }
tracing = "0.1"
metrics = "0.24"
async-trait = "0.1"
truefix-core = { path = "crates/truefix-core" }
truefix-dict = { path = "crates/truefix-dict" }
truefix-session = { path = "crates/truefix-session" }
truefix-transport = { path = "crates/truefix-transport" }
truefix-config = { path = "crates/truefix-config" }
truefix-store = { path = "crates/truefix-store" }
truefix-log = { path = "crates/truefix-log" }

[profile.release]
# Production builds: keep debug info off, optimize. Benchmarks live in benches/.
opt-level = 3

# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

## TrueFix — Complete Current-State Map (as of June 30, 2026)

A QuickFIX/J-parity FIX engine in Rust, async-first on tokio, TLS via rustls, typed errors (thiserror), workspace MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, and per-crate `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds. All 9 crates are at version `0.0.0`, `publish = false`. The README still claims "pre-implementation (design complete)" but the code is clearly substantially implemented through stage S8/S9.

### Workspace inventory (9 crates, all under `crates/`)
truefix-core, truefix-dict, truefix-session, truefix-store, truefix-log, truefix-transport, truefix-config, truefix-at, truefix (facade). 84 `.rs` files total. Dependency layering: core → dict/session → transport; store & log are standalone; config is standalone.

### 1. truefix-core (`src/lib.rs`)
Modules: `codec` (encode.rs/decode.rs/mod.rs), `cracker`, `error`, `factory`, `field`, `field_map`, `framing`, `group`, `message`, `tags`. Public surface: `Field`, `FieldMap`, `Group`, `Message`, `encode`/`decode`, `frame_length`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageFactory`, `DecodeError`, `FieldError`, `SOH`. SOH codec computes/verifies BodyLength(9)+CheckSum(10). Repeating-group structure modeled here; dictionary-driven group parsing deferred to dict. Has a `benches/codec.rs` bench.

### 2. truefix-dict — DUAL-TRACK dictionary (build.rs codegen + runtime)
- **build.rs**: Reads the SAME normalized `.fixdict` sources the runtime loads; for each of 9 versions generates a `*_DICT_HASH` (FNV-1a content hash) + a `pub mod {version}_msgs` with per-MsgType `&str` constants. The runtime asserts its parsed hash equals the generated one (proven by the `dual_track` test). Codegen is **per-version MsgType constants + hash only** — NOT typed message structs (the lib.rs doc explicitly says "Full typed dispatch over the generated messages arrives with the dictionary codegen (Stage S4)" but it has NOT yet happened).
- **Normalized dictionary sources present** (`dict-src/normalized/`): FIX40, FIX41, FIX42, FIX43, FIX44, FIXT11, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2 = **9 versions**. ✅ FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT 1.1. ❌ **No "FIX Latest"** (no such file/source).
- `model.rs`: `DataDictionary`, `FieldDef`, `FieldType` (16 types incl. UtcTimestamp/MonthYear/etc.), `MessageDef` (required/optional tags), `RejectReason` enum (codes 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,11), `ValidationError` (session vs business flag), `ValidationOptions` (5 toggles).
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate()` — field-membership, format, enum, required-field, UDF handling (tags ≥5000); unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject, else session reject (two rejection layers FR-C4).
- `parser.rs`, `fixt.rs` (`FixtDictionaries`), `hash.rs`. Loader fns: `load_fix40/41/42/43/44`, `load_fixt11`, `load_fix50/sp1/sp2`, plus `ALL_DICTS` const array.

### 3. truefix-session — sans-IO state machine (`src/state.rs`)
Modules: `admin`, `application`, `config`, `schedule`, `session_id`, `state`, `tags`, `time_util`. Public: `Action`, `Event`, `Session`, `SessionState` (Disconnected/AwaitingLogon/LoggedOn/AwaitingLogout), `SessionStatus`, `Role`, `SessionConfig`, `SessionId`, `Schedule`, `Application`.
- **SessionConfig keys** (programmatic): begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval, role, **reset_on_logon, reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num (789), enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed (369), check_latency, max_latency, logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule**.
- Implemented session semantics: logon/heartbeat/test-request/logout, **inbound seq validation (too-high→ResendRequest, too-low→Logout+disconnect)**, **ResendRequest handling with chunked resend (`resend_request_chunk_size`)**, **SequenceReset/gap-fill (PossDup/OrigSendingTime)**, **ResetSeqNumFlag (141=Y) on logon**, **NextExpectedMsgSeqNum (789) gap-fill-on-logon**, **LastMsgSeqNumProcessed (369) stamping**, **CheckLatency/MaxLatency** (SendingTime accuracy → logout), in-memory sent-message store + out-of-order queue, store seeding for restart continuity.
- `time_util.rs`: timestamp formatted as `YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss` — **millisecond precision only**. There is NO sub-millisecond / configurable precision; `TimeStampPrecision` config key is `Recognized` (pending) only.

### 4. truefix-store — `MessageStore` trait + 5 backends

# TrueFix cargo workspace. See specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md for the layered design.
[workspace]
resolver = "2"
members = ["crates/*"]

[workspace.package]
edition = "2021"
rust-version = "1.96"
license = "Apache-2.0 OR MIT"
repository = "https://github.com/zhangjiayin/truefix"
authors = ["Zhang Jiayin"]

# Lint policy (Constitution Principle I). `unsafe_code = forbid` is global; the
# critical-path clippy denies (unwrap/expect/panic/indexing) are applied per-crate
# on non-test builds via `#![cfg_attr(not(test), deny(...))]` in each lib.rs.
[workspace.lints.rust]
unsafe_code = "forbid"

# Shared dependency versions (single source of truth). Member crates opt in with
# `<dep>.workspace = true` as each stage needs them; unused entries are harmless.
[workspace.dependencies]

tokio-rustls = "0.26"

response.output:
# TrueFix

> A production-grade FIX protocol engine in Rust, at feature parity with QuickFIX/J — first-class
> acceptor/sell-side support, a dual-track data dictionary, and FIX conformance (acceptance tests) as the
> release gate.

[![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-Apache--2.0%20OR%20MIT-blue.svg)](#license)
![Status](https://img.shields.io/badge/status-pre--implementation%20(design%20complete)-orange.svg)
![Rust](https://img.shields.io/badge/rust-edition%202021-informational.svg)

**Status**: 🚧 Pre-implementation. The specification, plan, and task breakdown are complete and committed
as the project baseline; engine code has not started. See [`specs/001-fix-engine-parity/`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/).

## Why TrueFix

Existing Rust FIX libraries are either buy-side only or pre-1.0 and not production-trusted. TrueFix aims
to be the first Rust FIX engine that is:

- **Production-ready** — stable, documented public API; no `panic`/`unwrap`/`expect` on critical paths;
  typed, recoverable errors; structured observability (session state, sequence numbers, connection health).
- **Acceptor-first** — the sell-side/acceptor is a first-class citizen, on equal footing with the
  initiator (multi-session, dynamic sessions).
- **Conformance-gated** — protocol correctness is verified by a ported FIX **Acceptance Test (AT)** suite,
  which is the hard release gate.

The north-star goal is **full feature parity with QuickFIX/J**, delivered in one pass.

## Supported FIX versions (target)

FIX 4.0 / 4.1 / 4.2 / 4.3 / 4.4 / 5.0 / 5.0SP1 / 5.0SP2 and **FIXT 1.1** (with separate transport and
application dictionaries; `DefaultApplVerID` resolution).

## Architecture

Async-first engine on **tokio** (TLS via **rustls**), organized as a layered cargo workspace:

| Crate | Responsibility |
|-------|----------------|
| `truefix-core` | Field / FieldMap / Group / Message, field types, SOH codec, BodyLength/CheckSum, typed errors |
| `truefix-dict` | Data dictionary: **build-time codegen of typed messages + runtime `DataDictionary` validation** (dual-track, one source) |
| `truefix-session` | Session state machine, sequence management, admin messages, scheduling, resend/gap-fill, `NextExpectedMsgSeqNum`, chunked resend |
| `truefix-store` | `MessageStore` trait + Memory / File / CachedFile / SQL (sqlx) / Noop |
| `truefix-log` | `Log` trait + Screen / File / Tracing / Composite (message vs event streams) |
| `truefix-transport` | Initiator + Acceptor, multi/dynamic sessions, reconnect, socket options, rustls TLS |
| `truefix-config` | `SessionSettings`, `.cfg` parsing with `${name}` interpolation, programmatic config |
| `truefix` | Facade: re-exports + `Application` trait + `MessageCracker` |
| `truefix-at` | Ported Acceptance Test suite (release gate) |
| `examples/` | `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` |

### Dual-track data dictionary

Both tracks derive from **one normalized dictionary** (built from the FIX Trading Community's
Orchestra/Repository specs): build-time codegen gives compile-time-typed messages, while the runtime
`DataDictionary` gives version-agnostic validation, custom dictionaries, and user-defined fields — they
cannot diverge because they share a single source.

## Roadmap

Delivered in one pass to full parity, but internally **dependency-staged** with a verifiable checkpoint
per stage (see [`plan.md`](specs/001-fix-engine-parity/plan.md)):

```
S0 Workspace & CI ─▶ S1 Core codec ─▶ S2 Minimal session ─▶ S3 Recovery/resend ─▶ S4 Data dictionary
   ─▶ S5 Store/log ─▶ S6 Multi/dynamic/schedule/reconnect ─▶ S7 All versions + codegen
   ─▶ S8 TLS/auth/monitoring ─▶ S9 Acceptance Test suite (release gate)
```

## Testing & conformance

- Table-driven unit tests for the codec and session state machine.
- Two-process integration tests (real initiator ↔ acceptor) for handshake, heartbeat, resend, etc.
- The **AT suite** (`truefix-at`) ports the QuickFIX/QuickFIX-J acceptance scenarios as black-box
  behavior contracts; **all targeted FIX versions must pass to release**.
- CI runs `cargo fmt --check`, `cargo clippy -D warnings`, `cargo test`, `cargo deny`, and the AT matrix.

## Building & MSRV

```sh
cargo build --workspace
cargo fmt --all --check
cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings
cargo test --workspace
cargo deny check        # license/advisory gate (requires cargo-deny)
```

- **Edition**: 2021. **MSRV**: Rust **1.96** (declared in `workspace.package.rust-version`; build toolchain
  pinned in `rust-toolchain.toml`). The MSRV floor is raised only deliberately and noted here when it
  changes.
- `unsafe` code is forbidden workspace-wide (`unsafe_code = "forbid"`); `unwrap`/`expect`/`panic`/indexing
  are denied on non-test builds of every crate (Constitution Principle I).

## Governance

Development follows a written [constitution](.specify/memory/constitution.md) (v2.0.0). Core principles:
production-readiness, protocol correctness first, **License & provenance discipline**, dual-track
dictionary, test discipline, acceptor/initiator parity, and inventory-based completeness. On conflict:
License (absolute) → protocol correctness → production-readiness → feature count.

## License & provenance

Licensed under either of **Apache License, Version 2.0** ([LICENSE-APACHE](LICENSE-APACHE)) or
**MIT license** ([LICENSE-MIT](LICENSE-MIT)) at your option.

TrueFix is implemented from the FIX protocol specification itself. The QuickFIX (Go) and QuickFIX/J
(Java) projects were read **only** to understand architecture and protocol behavior; **no source code or
private data files were copied or translated**. Data dictionaries are derived from the FIX Trading
Community's official machine-readable specifications. This keeps TrueFix cleanly dual-licensable under
Apache-2.0 OR MIT.

Unless you explicitly state otherwise, any contribution intentionally submitted for inclusion in the work
by you shall be dual licensed as above, without any additional terms or conditions.

//! The Appendix A configuration-key registry (FR-I2, SC-004).
//!
//! Every QuickFIX/J configuration key in the spec's Appendix A baseline appears here with a
//! [`Stance`]: `Implemented` (recognized and honored), `Recognized` (parsed into settings;
//! behavior partial/pending in the current stage), or `Unsupported` (intentionally not supported,
//! with a reason). This guarantees no key is silently unrecognized.

/// How TrueFix treats a configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum Stance {
    /// Recognized and honored by the engine.
    Implemented,
    /// Recognized and parsed into settings; behavior is partial or pending.
    Recognized,
    /// Recognized but intentionally not supported in v1, with a reason.
    Unsupported(&'static str),
}

/// Metadata about one configuration key.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy)]
pub struct KeyInfo {
    /// The key name as it appears in a `.cfg`.
    pub name: &'static str,
    /// The Appendix A group it belongs to.
    pub group: &'static str,
    /// How TrueFix treats it.
    pub stance: Stance,
}

const fn k(name: &'static str, group: &'static str, stance: Stance) -> KeyInfo {
    KeyInfo {
        name,
        group,
        stance,
    }
}

use Stance::{Implemented as Impl, Recognized as Rec, Unsupported as Unsup};

/// The full Appendix A configuration-key baseline.
pub const APPENDIX_A_KEYS: &[KeyInfo] = &[
    // Session identity & type
    k("BeginString", "identity", Impl),
    k("SenderCompID", "identity", Impl),
    k("SenderSubID", "identity", Rec),
    k("SenderLocationID", "identity", Rec),
    k("TargetCompID", "identity", Impl),
    k("TargetSubID", "identity", Rec),
    k("TargetLocationID", "identity", Rec),
    k("SessionQualifier", "identity", Rec),
    k("ConnectionType", "identity", Impl),
    k("Description", "identity", Rec),
    k("DefaultApplVerID", "identity", Impl),
    // Dictionary & validation
    k("UseDataDictionary", "validation", Impl),
    k("DataDictionary", "validation", Impl),
    k("AppDataDictionary", "validation", Impl),
    k("TransportDataDictionary", "validation", Impl),
    k("ValidateFieldsOutOfOrder", "validation", Rec),
    k("ValidateFieldsHaveValues", "validation", Impl),
    k("ValidateUnorderedGroupFields", "validation", Rec),
    k("ValidateUserDefinedFields", "validation", Impl),
    k("ValidateIncomingMessage", "validation", Impl),
    k("ValidateSequenceNumbers", "validation", Impl),
    k("ValidateChecksum", "validation", Impl),
    k("AllowUnknownMsgFields", "validation", Impl),
    k("CheckLatency", "validation", Impl),
    k("MaxLatency", "validation", Impl),
    k("CheckCompID", "validation", Rec),
    k("RejectGarbledMessage", "validation", Rec),
    k("RejectInvalidMessage", "validation", Impl),
    k("RejectMessageOnUnhandledException", "validation", Rec),
    k("FirstFieldInGroupIsDelimiter", "validation", Rec),
    // Session behavior
    k("HeartBtInt", "session", Impl),
    k("HeartBeatTimeoutMultiplier", "session", Rec),
    k("DisableHeartBeatCheck", "session", Rec),
    k("TestRequestDelayMultiplier", "session", Rec),
    k("LogonTimeout", "session", Impl),
    k("LogoutTimeout", "session", Impl),
    k("LogonTag", "session", Rec),
    k("ResetOnLogon", "session", Impl),
    k("ResetOnLogout", "session", Impl),
    k("ResetOnDisconnect", "session", Impl),
    k("ResetOnError", "session", Rec),
    k("RefreshOnLogon", "session", Rec),
    k("PersistMessages", "session", Rec),
    k("ResendRequestChunkSize", "session", Impl),
    k("SendRedundantResendRequests", "session", Rec),
    k("ClosedResendInterval", "session", Rec),
    k("EnableLastMsgSeqNumProcessed", "session", Rec),
    k("EnableNextExpectedMsgSeqNum", "session", Impl),

args.result:
## TrueFix — Complete Current-State Map (as of June 30, 2026)

A QuickFIX/J-parity FIX engine in Rust, async-first on tokio, TLS via rustls, typed errors (thiserror), workspace MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, and per-crate `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds. All 9 crates are at version `0.0.0`, `publish = false`. The README still claims "pre-implementation (design complete)" but the code is clearly substantially implemented through stage S8/S9.

### Workspace inventory (9 crates, all under `crates/`)
truefix-core, truefix-dict, truefix-session, truefix-store, truefix-log, truefix-transport, truefix-config, truefix-at, truefix (facade). 84 `.rs` files total. Dependency layering: core → dict/session → transport; store & log are standalone; config is standalone.

### 1. truefix-core (`src/lib.rs`)
Modules: `codec` (encode.rs/decode.rs/mod.rs), `cracker`, `error`, `factory`, `field`, `field_map`, `framing`, `group`, `message`, `tags`. Public surface: `Field`, `FieldMap`, `Group`, `Message`, `encode`/`decode`, `frame_length`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageFactory`, `DecodeError`, `FieldError`, `SOH`. SOH codec computes/verifies BodyLength(9)+CheckSum(10). Repeating-group structure modeled here; dictionary-driven group parsing deferred to dict. Has a `benches/codec.rs` bench.

### 2. truefix-dict — DUAL-TRACK dictionary (build.rs codegen + runtime)
- **build.rs**: Reads the SAME normalized `.fixdict` sources the runtime loads; for each of 9 versions generates a `*_DICT_HASH` (FNV-1a content hash) + a `pub mod {version}_msgs` with per-MsgType `&str` constants. The runtime asserts its parsed hash equals the generated one (proven by the `dual_track` test). Codegen is **per-version MsgType constants + hash only** — NOT typed message structs (the lib.rs doc explicitly says "Full typed dispatch over the generated messages arrives with the dictionary codegen (Stage S4)" but it has NOT yet happened).
- **Normalized dictionary sources present** (`dict-src/normalized/`): FIX40, FIX41, FIX42, FIX43, FIX44, FIXT11, FIX50, FIX50SP1, FIX50SP2 = **9 versions**. ✅ FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT 1.1. ❌ **No "FIX Latest"** (no such file/source).
- `model.rs`: `DataDictionary`, `FieldDef`, `FieldType` (16 types incl. UtcTimestamp/MonthYear/etc.), `MessageDef` (required/optional tags), `RejectReason` enum (codes 0,1,2,3,4,5,6,11), `ValidationError` (session vs business flag), `ValidationOptions` (5 toggles).
- `validate.rs`: `DataDictionary::validate()` — field-membership, format, enum, required-field, UDF handling (tags ≥5000); unknown-but-structural MsgType → business reject, else session reject (two rejection layers FR-C4).
- `parser.rs`, `fixt.rs` (`FixtDictionaries`), `hash.rs`. Loader fns: `load_fix40/41/42/43/44`, `load_fixt11`, `load_fix50/sp1/sp2`, plus `ALL_DICTS` const array.

### 3. truefix-session — sans-IO state machine (`src/state.rs`)
Modules: `admin`, `application`, `config`, `schedule`, `session_id`, `state`, `tags`, `time_util`. Public: `Action`, `Event`, `Session`, `SessionState` (Disconnected/AwaitingLogon/LoggedOn/AwaitingLogout), `SessionStatus`, `Role`, `SessionConfig`, `SessionId`, `Schedule`, `Application`.
- **SessionConfig keys** (programmatic): begin_string, sender/target_comp_id, heartbeat_interval, role, **reset_on_logon, reset_on_logout, reset_on_disconnect, refresh_on_logon, resend_request_chunk_size, enable_next_expected_msg_seq_num (789), enable_last_msg_seq_num_processed (369), check_latency, max_latency, logon_timeout, logout_timeout, reconnect_interval, schedule**.
- Implemented session semantics: logon/heartbeat/test-request/logout, **inbound seq validation (too-high→ResendRequest, too-low→Logout+disconnect)**, **ResendRequest handling with chunked resend (`resend_request_chunk_size`)**, **SequenceReset/gap-fill (PossDup/OrigSendingTime)**, **ResetSeqNumFlag (141=Y) on logon**, **NextExpectedMsgSeqNum (789) gap-fill-on-logon**, **LastMsgSeqNumProcessed (369) stamping**, **CheckLatency/MaxLatency** (SendingTime accuracy → logout), in-memory sent-message store + out-of-order queue, store seeding for restart continuity.
- `time_util.rs`: timestamp formatted as `YYYYMMDD-HH:MM:SS.sss` — **millisecond precision only**. There is NO sub-millisecond / configurable precision; `TimeStampPrecision` config key is `Recognized` (pending) only.

### 4. truefix-store — `MessageStore` trait + 5 backends
`MemoryStore`, `FileStore`, `CachedFileStore`, `NoopStore`, and **`SqlStore` (behind `sql` feature, SQLite via sqlx)** — JDBC-equivalent: `seqnums` table + `messages` BLOB table. `StoreConfig` enum + `build_store()` factory. Trait: next_sender/target_seq, set, save, get(begin,end), reset. ❌ No MongoDB. Sleepycat/JE explicitly `Unsupported`.

### 5. truefix-log — `Log` trait + 5 backends
`ScreenLog`, `FileLog`, `TracingLog`, `CompositeLog` (fan-out), and **`SqlLog` (behind `sql` feature)** — writes `log_messages`/`log_events` tables via a **background writer task** bridging the sync `Log` trait to async sqlx. `LogConfig` enum + `build_log()`. Message/event stream separation (FR-H2). SLF4J category keys explicitly `Unsupported` (uses `tracing` instead).

### 6. truefix-transport — tokio Initiator + Acceptor
`SocketOptions` (tcp_no_delay), `Services` (store/log/socket_options/**Monitor**/**validator**=(DataDictionary,ValidationOptions)), `SessionHandle`, `Monitor`.
- **Single-session**: `connect_initiator`, `connect_initiator_with`, `connect_initiator_tls` (rustls, SNI), `Acceptor` (static config per connection).
- **Multi/dynamic sessions (S6)**: `AcceptorBuilder` with `with_session` (route by SessionID from the Logon), `with_dynamic_template` (AcceptorTemplate/DynamicSession), `allow_remotes` (AllowedRemoteAddresses allow-listing), `with_tls` (TlsAcceptor), `with_services`.
- **Reconnecting initiator**: `connect_initiator_reconnecting` (uses `reconnect_interval`, loops until stopped).
- **Scheduled initiator**: `run_scheduled_initiator` — connects only while `Schedule::is_in_session`; on entering a window resets the store (FR-E3), on leaving logs out.
- **Monitor (FR-L)** = the JMX-equivalent surface: live `status()`/`is_connected()`/`sessions()` snapshot + `force_logout()`/`reset()`/`send_app()` control actions.
- Reconnect, restart sequence-number continuity via store, TLS (rcgen in dev-deps for test certs).
- ❌ **No failover / multi-endpoint / alternate-backup-endpoint support** (only single-address reconnect). ❌ **No proxy protocol / HTTP/SOCKS proxy** — all `Proxy*` keys are `Unsupported`.

### 7. truefix-config — `.cfg` parsing + Appendix A key registry

Exposes `Application`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageFactory`, `connect_initiator`, `Acceptor`, `SessionHandle`, etc. 4 examples: `executor`, `banzai`, `multi_acceptor`, `ordermatch` (the ordermatch example uses `Monitor` + `AcceptorBuilder` + dynamic template + `send_app` to fill crossing orders).

### Specific feature questions answered
- **DoNotSend / RejectLogon / FieldNotFound / UnsupportedMessageType / IncorrectTagValue error TYPES**: ❌ None exist as named types. The QuickFIX exceptions are NOT ported as error enums. Closest equivalents: `Application::from_admin` returns `Result<(), String>` (a `Err` tears the session down = effective RejectLogon); dict uses `RejectReason` enum (InvalidTagNumber/RequiredTagMissing/TagNotDefinedForMessageType/UndefinedTag/TagSpecifiedWithoutValue/ValueIsIncorrect/IncorrectDataFormat/InvalidMsgType) + `ValidationError` (business flag = UnsupportedMessageType-ish). DecodeError/FieldError are the only typed error enums.
- **JMX / monitoring**: ✅ `Monitor` is the JMX-equivalent (status snapshot + force_logout/reset/send_app controls) — NOT a JMX MBean server.
- **Proxy protocol / failover / multi-endpoint**: ❌ All absent/Unsupported.
- **MongoDB**: ❌ Not present.
- **FIX Latest**: ❌ Not present (only FIX 4.0–5.0SP2 + FIXT 1.1).
- **Timestamp precision**: millisecond only; `TimeStampPrecision` key Recognized/pending. **ResetSeqTime**: not present as a config key (only ResetSeqNumFlag/ResetOnLogon/Logout/Disconnect). **FileStoreSync**: key Recognized/pending, not honored. **HeartBtIntOverride**: not present; acceptor does adopt the client's HeartBtInt from Logon (tag 108) at runtime.
- **MessageCracker / typed dispatch**: `MessageCracker` is a **bare trait skeleton** (`fn crack(&mut self, &Message) -> Result<(), Self::Error>`). No typed per-MsgType dispatch macro/impl yet — explicitly deferred to codegen (S4), which has not produced typed message structs.
- **ApplicationFunctionalAdapter**: ❌ Not present. `Application` is the only callback trait (on_create/on_logon/on_logout/to_admin/from_admin/to_app/from_app, all async, default no-ops).
- **New examples vs. README**: README lists executor/banzai/multi_acceptor/ordermatch — these match the actual `examples/` dir, so no new examples beyond the documented four.

### Evidence (key files read)
- Workspace `Cargo.toml`, `CLAUDE.md`, `README.md`
- All 9 crate `Cargo.toml` + all 9 `lib.rs`
- core: `error.rs`, `cracker.rs`, `factory.rs`
- dict: `build.rs`, `model.rs`, `validate.rs`; `dict-src/normalized/` listing (9 files)
- session: `config.rs`, `application.rs`, `state.rs`, `schedule.rs`, `time_util.rs`
- store: `sql.rs`; log: `sql.rs`
- transport: full `lib.rs` (Monitor, AcceptorBuilder, reconnecting/scheduled initiator, TLS)
- config: `keys.rs` (full Appendix A registry), `lib.rs` (parser)
- at: `runner.rs`, `scenarios.rs`
- example: `ordermatch.rs`
- Grep confirmed absence of DoNotSend/RejectLogon/FieldNotFound/UnsupportedMessageType/IncorrectTagValue/ApplicationFunctionalAdapter/MongoDB/FixLatest/failover/ResetSeqTime/HeartBtIntOverride across all crates (only "JMX-equivalent" comment in transport).

Next steps:
The investigation is complete — no further exploration needed. If the caller wants to act on gaps: the most notable NOT-YET-IMPLEMENTED features (vs. QuickFIX/J parity) are (1) typed MessageCracker dispatch / generated message structs in truefix-dict codegen, (2) the named exception types (DoNotSend/RejectLogon/FieldNotFound/UnsupportedMessageType/IncorrectTagValue), (3) MongoDB & FIX Latest & proxy/failover/multi-endpoint, (4) configurable TimeStampPrecision / FileStoreSync / ResetSeqTime, and (5) expanding the AT suite from 19 scenarios toward the full 73. The `TimeStampPrecision`, `FileStoreSync`, and related keys are already Registered in keys.rs awaiting implementation.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when finding files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 1000). Use to limit output for broad patterns.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

file_filtering_options.description: Optional: Whether to respect ignore patterns from .gitignore or .geminiignore

respect_git_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .gitignore patterns when listing files. Only available in git repositories. Defaults to true.

respect_gemini_ignore.description: Optional: Whether to respect .geminiignore patterns when listing files. Defaults to true.

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text, images (PNG, JPG, GIF, WEBP, SVG, BMP), and PDF files. For images, videos or other multimedia files, use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool if available.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command as `bash -c <command>`. Command is executed as a subprocess that leads its own process group.

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- This tool defaults to non-interactive execution. Set `interactive` to `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for ordinary one-shot commands such as `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact bash command to execute as `bash -c <command>`. Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

run_in_background.description: Set to true to run this command in the background. Returns immediately with task metadata and an output file path.

interactive.description: Request an attached interactive shell session. Use `true` only for a bare REPL such as `python` or `node`, or for a password prompt. Leave it unset for normal one-shot commands like `git status`, `npm test`, `pytest`, or `rg foo`.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. Returns the lines containing matches, along with their file paths and line numbers.

pattern.description: The regular expression (regex) pattern to search for within file contents (e.g., 'function\s+myFunction', 'import\s+\{.*\}\s+from\s+.*').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

include.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 20, max: 150). Values above 150 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches.

## TrueFix — Complete Current-State Map (as of June 30, 2026)

A QuickFIX/J-parity FIX engine in Rust, async-first on tokio, TLS via rustls, typed errors (thiserror), workspace MSRV 1.96, `unsafe_code = forbid` workspace-wide, and per-crate `deny(clippy::unwrap_used/expect_used/panic/indexing_slicing)` on non-test builds. All 9 crates are at version `0.0.0`, `publish = false`. The README still claims "pre-implementation (design complete)" but the code is clearly substantially implemented through stage S8/S9.

### Workspace inventory (9 crates, all under `crates/`)
truefix-core, truefix-dict, truefix-session, truefix-store, truefix-log, truefix-transport, truefix-config, truefix-at, truefix (facade). 84 `.rs` files total. Dependency layering: core → dict/session → transport; store & log are standalone; config is standalone.

### 1. truefix-core (`src/lib.rs`)
Modules: `codec` (encode.rs/decode.rs/mod.rs), `cracker`, `error`, `factory`, `field`, `field_map`, `framing`, `group`, `message`, `tags`. Public surface: `Field`, `FieldMap`, `Group`, `Message`, `encode`/`decode`, `frame_length`, `MessageCracker`, `MessageFactory`, `DecodeError`, `FieldError`, `SOH`. SOH codec computes/verifies BodyLength(9)+CheckSum(10). Repeating-group structure modeled here; dictionary-driven group parsing deferred to dict. Has a `benches/codec.rs` bench.

### 2. truefix-dict — DUAL-TRACK dictionary (build.rs codegen + runtime)
- **build.rs**: Reads the SAME normalized `.fixdict` sources the runtime loads; for each of 9 versions generates a `*_DICT_HASH` (FNV-1a content hash) + a `pub mod {version}_msgs` with per-MsgType `&str` constants. The runtime asserts its parsed hash equals the generated one (proven by the `dual_track` test). Codegen is **per-version MsgType constants + hash only** — NOT typed message structs (the lib.rs doc explicitly says "Full typed dispatch over the generated messages arrives with the dictionary codegen (Stage S4)" but it has NOT yet happened).
